titleStmt.title: SII 40.171: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 171. (A.R. No. B. 306 of 1931.) Nāgulavaram, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a pillar buried in the prākāra of the Chennakēśavasvāmi temple in the village.

Mutilated at the end. Refers to Oruṅgallu as the king's capital and to Gaṇḍapeṇḍāra Gaṅgayya-Sāhiṇi as governing the whole country from Pānuṅgallu to Mārjavaḍa. Further states that a Kāyastha named Nāmadēva-Paṇḍita (described as Śrī Karaṇādhipati) and a subordinate of the chief set up the god Chennakēśvaradēva, named after his father at Durgi and approached the king (evidently for some endowment for the worship of the deity).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti samadhigata paṁcamahāśa
bda mahāmaṁḍalēśvara parama
māhēśvara patihita carita vi
naya vibhūṣaṇa śrīyanmakoṁ
ḍapuravarādhīśvarulaina śrīmanma
hā maṁḍalēśvara kākatiyya ga
ṇapatidēva mahārājulu
Ōrugallu nijarājadhānigā
sukhasaṁkathā vinōdaṁbu
laṁ brithvirājyaṁbu sēyu
cuṁḍaṁgāṁ datpādapadmōpa
jīviyaina śrīmatu gaṁḍapeṁ
ḍāra gaṁggayya sāhiṇi hā
nuṁgallu mōdalugā mārji
vāḍadāṁkā sukha saṁkathā
vinōdaṁbula rājyaṁbu sē
yucuṁḍaṁgāṁ datpāda
padmōpajīviyunsakala rājya
samuddaruṁḍunu śrīkaraṇa
dhipatiyunuṁ gāyasthakula
vāddhivaddhana sudhākaruṁḍu
nunaina śrī nāmadēva paṁḍitu
lu pallināṁṭilōni rdugi paṭṭa
namunaṁdu tamataṁḍri sō
ma paṁḍitula pēranu caṁkēśva
radēvaraṁ bratiṣṭha sēsi gaṇapa
tidēva mahārājuṁgāri kā
ruṇyamu naḍasi mūṟurāya
jagadāḷapaḍagānnuchalamerttu
gaṁḍabirudūṁdeccenaṁ cēko
ni saṁtōṣiṁciyunnayavana
raṁbuna dēvara kāruṇyamuna
dugyanu caṁkēśvaradēvaraṁ bri
tiṣṭha sēstinani vinnapamu sēsi
nānu Ādēvaraku namaskariṁ
ci . svasti śrī śakavarṣaṁbulu The
Digital edition of SII 40.171 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
